gene_region.label: VDR transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical VDR genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: VDR encodes the vitamin D receptor, a ligand-regulated nuclear receptor. This bundle emphasizes vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local VDR bundle is vitamin D receptor signaling oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: VDR variants can alter or tag vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
condition_research_overview: Bone mineral and calcium homeostasis context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: VDR methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for VDR.
Because VDR biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use VDR promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription.
Bone mineral and calcium homeostasis context

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000111424: VDR coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: VDR biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: VDR: vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription
biorender_visuals.template_title: Intracellular Layout - Receptor Signaling from Plasma Membrane to Nucleus (2)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a VDR vitamin D receptor signaling thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription and Bone mineral and calcium homeostasis context, Immune and infection association research. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs2228570 (VDR FokI) is curated as a VDR marker for Bone mineral and calcium homeostasis context, Immune and infection association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag vitamin D hormone signaling, calcium/phosphate homeostasis, immune modulation, bone biology, and nuclear receptor transcription.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Bone mineral and calcium homeostasis context
research_context: Use this marker as a VDR pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs2228570 (VDR FokI) with VDR methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs2228570 (VDR FokI) or linked VDR locus genotype states
